extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. Property G(opted_out implies not sent): whenever opted_out is true, sent must be false.

The transition [t_resume_glitch] on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 1): sent := true directly sets sent true while opted_out is true. To enable it, we need opted_out=true and ticks>=1.

Trace from initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0):
1. tick: t_tick fires (guard true), ticks 0<1 so ticks:=1, sent:=false -> (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=1).
2. optout: t_optout fires, opted_out:=true, sent:=false -> (opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=1).
3. send_attempt: now opted_out=true AND ticks>=1, so t_resume_glitch is enabled and sets sent:=true -> (opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=1).

This reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating opted_out implies not sent. The counterexample uses 3 events, within the 5-event bound. (Even though t_send_blocked is also enabled on this send_attempt and would set sent:=false, t_resume_glitch is a reachable enabled transition producing a violating state, so an execution exists that violates the property. The transition is literally named "resume_glitch" — it is the intended bug.) Property is violated.
